title: Epigram On Hearing A Clergyman Preach A Dull Sermon In A Loud, Shrill Voice
content:
В В В В Still, still his bell-like voice rings through my head;
В В В В Yet not one bright thought cheers my mental view;
В В В В O! would that I were deaf, asleep, or dead!
В В В В Ye marble statues! how I envy you!

В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В *В В В В В В В В *В В В В В В В В *В В В В В В В В *

В В В В To hear him preach the Methodistic creed,
В В В В What eager crowds to Ranter's chapel speed!
В В В В His eloquence the harden'd sinner frightens;
В В В В Like heaven itself says Fame, he thunders, lightens.
В В В В I go to hear him; Fame has made a blunder;
В В В В I see no lightning, though I hear the thunder.

В В В В For flowery sermons Doctor Drudge
В В В В В В В В Of preachers at the top is;
В В В В If from their influence we may judge,
В В В В В В В В His flowers are only poppies.

В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В *В В В В В В В В *В В В В В В В В *В В В В В В В В *

В В В В Sir! you're both fool and knave! to Frank, Blunt cries
В В В В I know I am, Sir, Frank to Blunt replies:
В В В В Now, in self-knowledge if all knowledge lies,
В В В В A fool, like Frank, must be extremely wise!

В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В *В В В В В В В В *В В В В В В В В *В В В В В В В В *

В В В В Vice is a mouse-trap, pleasure is the bait,
В В В В Like mice, enticing mortals to their fate;
В В В В And of this truth experience leaves no doubt;
В В В В 'Tis far more easy to get in than out.

В В В В Old maids their spleen on marriage vent;
В В В В В В В В The reason would you know?
В В В В 'Tis not, that others are made wives,
В В В В В В В В But that they can't be so.

В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В *В В В В В В В В *В В В В В В В В *В В В В В В В В *

В В В В How grave he looks! how mighty wise!
В В В В He seems Minerva's sacred bird:
В В В В He speaks! our ears refute our eyes
В В В В The cackling of a goose is heard.

В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В *В В В В В В В В *В В В В В В В В *В В В В В В В В *

В В В В How came that Jew, deform'd and old,
В В В В To wed the youthful, fair Coquette?
В В В В Ben had a purse well-stored with gold!
В В В В He caught her in't; 'twas Hymen's net!
В В В В Flirtilla's teeth, well-form'd and white,
В В В В Were Hymen's pincers, and could bite!
В В В В When the Royal Exchange to the flames fell a prey,
В В В В All the Monarchs and Queens from their niches were thrown;
В В В В Lackaday! on the pavement in fragments they lay,
В В В В Every one except Charley the Second alone.
В В В В Strange event! O my Muse! to blind mortals below
В В В В Clear this mystery which none but immortals can know.
В В В В "Cytherea and Momus pray'd Vulcan to spare
В В В В The blithe, amorous King: Vulcan granted their prayer."
meaning:
Thomas Oldham's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Epigram On Hearing A Clergyman Preach A Dull Sermon In A Loud, Shrill Voice"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
